name: simple_foot_spatial_spec
purpose: Minimal spatial knowledge for foot-bone visual reasoning. Keep this file simple and human-verifiable.

metatarsals.location: middle long bones between toes and ankle/tarsals

stable_object_space_facts: Toes are distal to the ankle region.
Ankle region is proximal to the toes.

DorsalTopDown.meaning: View from the top/dorsal side of the foot looking toward the plantar side.
DorsalTopDown.simple_check: If the top of the foot dominates and side depth is minimal, this is likely dorsal top-down.

PlantarBottomUp.meaning: View from the bottom/plantar side of the foot looking toward the dorsal side.
PlantarBottomUp.simple_check: If the sole/bottom side dominates, this is likely plantar bottom-up.

MedialSide.meaning: View from the big-toe/medial side looking toward the little-toe/lateral side.
MedialSide.simple_check: If the big-toe side dominates, this is likely medial side view.

LateralSide.meaning: View from the little-toe/lateral side looking toward the big-toe/medial side.
LateralSide.simple_check: If the little-toe side dominates, this is likely lateral side view.

DistalToeEnd.meaning: View from the toe/distal end looking toward the ankle/proximal end.
DistalToeEnd.simple_check: If the toes are closest to the camera, this is likely distal toe-end view.

ProximalAnkleEnd.meaning: View from the ankle/proximal end looking toward the toes/distal end.
ProximalAnkleEnd.simple_check: If the ankle/tarsal region is closest to the camera, this is likely proximal ankle-end view.

image_space_notes.definition: Image-space describes where things appear on the 2D screen. It can change because of camera roll or image rotation.
examples: Toes appearing at the bottom does not by itself define the anatomical view.
A dorsal top-down view can have toes at the bottom, top, left, or right depending on image roll.
If toes are at bottom and ankle is at top, the image is oriented with distal direction downward.
If toes and ankle swap screen positions but the visible surface is the same, it may be a roll/orientation issue, not a new view.

minimal_reasoning_rules.instruction: Use canonical_views to decide which anatomical view the target asks for.
Compare the visual diagnosis against the target view's should_see and should_not_dominate fields.
Do not confuse image-space position, such as toes at bottom, with object-space anatomical view.
Only change camera/view parameters needed to fix the mismatch. Do not change rendering style unless explicitly requested.

expected_agent_loop: Map target to one canonical view.
Check what is missing or wrong using should_see and should_not_dominate.
Decide whether the issue is main view angle, image roll, or too much oblique/side depth.
